🦜 Parrot Runner - AI Pipeline Observability

An AI generates each game asset, automatic rules and a second AI check it, and anything uncertain goes to a human. This page shows the actual decision trail, every quality gate, the real-play loop, and what it costs - readable whether or not you write code. 31 run(s) logged. Latest judge: mock (claude-opus-4-8). Updated 2026-06-24 15:19 UTC.
🟢 deterministic code (exact) 🔵 AI (creative/judgment) 🟣 AI judge (taste) 🙋 human (final call) 🏆 real-play winner

Brief requirements

RequirementStatusNeededEvidence
Functional core loopmet ✓start → fly & collect → end with scorestart screen, scrolling collision-scoring flight, end card with final score
Controllable parrotmet ✓virtual joystick OR keyboardboth - keyboard (arrows / WASD) + on-screen joystick
AI-generated collectible (scores points)met ✓at least 1fruit.png - AI-generated, judged & accepted, +points on pickup
AI-generated tree (environment)met ✓at least 1tree.png - AI-generated, judged & accepted, scattered along the path

How it works

1. Generate
An AI draws the sprite from a saved prompt
image provider (Pollinations / mock)
→
2. Validate
Hard rules check it is a usable image
PNG · square · transparent · ≤150KB
→
3. Judge
A second AI scores it on 5 quality points
LLM vision judge (Gemini / Claude / mock)
→
4. Retry
Low scores get feedback and another try
feedback appended to the prompt
→
5. Escalate
If still unsure, a human decides
human-in-the-loop, never a guess

What we use AI for - and what we deliberately keep as code

The core decision rule: AI for judgment/creative tasks, plain code for deterministic ones. Knowing where not to use AI is the point.
TaskHandled byWhy
Sprite & background artAICreative, high-variability - taste matters, many valid outputs
Asset quality gradingAI judgeSubjective rubric - a genuine judgment call
Collision, scoring, loop, physicsCodeDeterministic - must be exact, fast, identical every run
Validation gatesCodeFail-loud rules - no LLM variance, no guessing
Background acceptanceCode (validate)A backdrop that passes image checks is fine - don't over-apply AI
Provenance / observabilityCodeHashing & audit must be deterministic

Agent config & versions (rollback-ready)

The agent runtime is separate from its config: behaviour is changed by editing one versioned file (agents.config.json) - a reviewable PR and a git-revertable rollback - not code. Prompt text is versioned as code in prompts/*.md, and each run records the version it used (audit).
Config version1.2.0
Ownerdkauzi
Last audited2026-06-24
Image providerpollinations
Judge modelsgemini-2.5-flash (free) · claude-opus-4-8 (premium)
Thresholdsaccept ≥ 18/25 · min criterion 3 · max attempts 3
Prompt versionsfruit v0.2 · tree v0.2 · background v0.1 · ground v0.1

The agentic loop - how the decision was actually made

Not "trust me." This is the real decision trail from the logs: Agent A generates, Agent B (the AI judge) scores and accepts or rejects, and on a reject Agent A re-generates from B's feedback until it passes - or a human is called. Watch an asset that was rejected and then re-generated to pass.
parrot - accepted first pass
  1. Attempt 1 Agent A generates → Agent B judges 20/25 → ACCEPTED ✓
    “mock grade for parrot”
fruit - 2 loops - rejected, then re-generated
  1. Attempt 1 Agent A generates → Agent B judges 17/25 → REJECTED → Agent A regenerates
    “mock grade for fruit”
  2. Attempt 2 Agent A generates → Agent B judges 20/25 → ACCEPTED ✓
    “mock grade for fruit”
tree - 2 loops - rejected, then re-generated
  1. Attempt 1 Agent A generates → Agent B judges 17/25 → REJECTED → Agent A regenerates
    “mock grade for tree”
  2. Attempt 2 Agent A generates → Agent B judges 20/25 → ACCEPTED ✓
    “mock grade for tree”

Judge calibration - who watches the judge?

The judge is an LLM, so it can drift (a model swap silently moves the quality bar). A golden set of labeled cases - real good sprites that must pass, plus a deliberately bad image that must be rejected - checks the judge's agreement with known-correct verdicts. A drop in agreement means the evaluator regressed, caught here before it corrupts future grades.
100%
judge agreement
gemini:gemini-2.5-flash
Golden caseExpectedJudge saidMatch
fruit (real, good)acceptaccept✓
coconut (real, good)acceptaccept✓
banana (real, good)acceptaccept✓
gray square (deliberately bad)rejectreject✓

The end product - tests & visual QA

Two checks on the actual built game: functional Playwright tests (does it load and run), and an AI visual judge that looks at a real gameplay screenshot (does it LOOK right) - catching what functional tests can't, like the sprite-background bug.
live gameplay (animated)
AI visual verdict: looks good ✓ (null/5) - AI judge skipped (no key)
Functional game testStatus
captures a gameplay screenshot (visual artifact)pass ✓
loads with no console or page errorspass ✓
game loop advances (not frozen)pass ✓
WebGL context is healthy (actually rendering)pass ✓
perspective/projection is correct (sane FOV, no stretch)pass ✓
CTA fires through mraid.open at game endpass ✓

Provenance - proof of AI origin

Each sprite is fingerprinted (SHA-256) when the AI generates it. This re-checks the shipped file still matches - proving it came from the AI generator and was not swapped for hand-drawn or procedural art. Verification + observability.
AssetAI generatorGraded byScoreSHA-256Verified
parrotpollinationsgemini:gemini-2.5-flash23/25736a904d5638f59b…verified ✓
fruitpollinationsgemini:gemini-2.5-flash22/25f476a87a1bbfbc5b…verified ✓
treepollinationsmock:mock20/2503bc487861947eda…verified ✓
coconutpollinationsmock:mock22/25f936b682e966f722…verified ✓
bananapollinationsmock:mock20/25e82338c964532615…verified ✓

Operational insights - what caused the most rework

Aggregated across all runs. Biggest source of re-work: Judge rejects → regenerated - where to focus tooling next. (Validation rejects are good: bad assets caught early and never shipped - fail loud, not silent.)
CauseCount
Generation errors (provider/network)0
Validation rejects (caught early)0
Judge rejects → regenerated17
Human escalations12
What was actually hard: not the code - the judgment calls. Where to draw the AI/deterministic line, why the AI judge can't be the sole arbiter (it gave a buggy frame 5/5), and finding the camera bug the functional tests passed straight through. The build is cheap; the decisions are the work.

💸 Cost & tokens (AI is metered)

Every AI call is counted. This whole pipeline runs on free tiers, so spend is ~$0 - but it's tracked so it scales honestly.
MeterValue
AI image generations0 (Pollinations, $0)
AI judge calls73 (Gemini free tier)
Est. judge tokens~23,360
Total cost$0.0000

🔄 The data flywheel (the whole loop)

In plain words: we make the art, check it, ship a version, watch how people actually play it, then make more of whatever plays best - and go round again. Each loop makes the next batch better. Every step below is built and observable on this page.
📝
Prompt
versioned, like code
→
🎨
Make art
an AI paints the asset
→
🟢
Check rules
format, transparent, sized
→
🔵
AI judges
scores it like a designer
→
🔁
Fix & retry
feedback rewrites the prompt
→
🙋
Human if unsure
never a guess
→
🕹️
Ship variant
validated playable
→
🎯
Watch real play
score · pickups · camera
→
📊
Rank winners
by engagement
↩ Loop closes here: the winning variant feeds straight back into Make art - generate more of what plays best. Deterministic gates everywhere · AI only for judgment · human is the final judge · no guessing.

✅ Functional tests & regression (re-run on every code change)

CI runs these on every push, so a code change can't silently break play. Unit = game logic; e2e = the real built game in a browser; regression = the golden eval guarding the AI judge from drift.
SuiteResultCases
🟢 Unit (logic)11/11 ✅within: overlapping points are a hit · within: points beyond reach are not a hit · within: exactly at reach is inclusive · distanceSq matches the math · collect returns indices of all touched items · collect returns empty when nothing is near · comboMultiplier grows then caps · continuesCombo respects the time window · pickupValue applies and rounds the multiplier · isRunComplete triggers at the distance limit · runProgress is clamped to 0..1
🔵 e2e (built game)6/6 ✅captures a gameplay screenshot (visual artifact) · loads with no console or page errors · game loop advances (not frozen) · WebGL context is healthy (actually rendering) · perspective/projection is correct (sane FOV, no stretch) · CTA fires through mraid.open at game end
🟣 Regression (judge drift)-/4golden eval (judge drift guard)

🎯 Closing the loop - real-play performance (the north-star)

The asset rubric asks "does it look good?". This asks "which variant actually performs in play?". Each variant is run repeatedly; the game's window.__telemetry is aggregated into engagement. In production this same shape is fed by the ad-network's performance webhook - that's the data flywheel: real play feeds back into what the pipeline generates next. Metric: engagement = avgScore + avgPickups×5; 9 runs.
VariantEngagementShareAvg scoreAvg pickupsRuns
🏆 rush5467%3933
classic22.528%161.33
zen4.56%30.33
Recommendation: Generate more variants in the "rush" direction; it leads on real play (67% engagement share).

Champion vs Challenger - safe prompt rollout

A candidate prompt is shadow-run beside the live one, graded the same way, and only recommended for promotion when it clearly wins. The system never auto-swaps - the human approves the version bump. Judge: mock (deterministic-only).
PromptAvg scoreSamplesPasses deterministic gate
champion (live)713✅
challenger (candidate)803✅
Δ +9 (margin 3) → PROMOTE challenger. Recommendation only - a human approves the prompt-version bump (human remains the judge).

All validation gates

Every automatic check in the project. "live" = result from this run; "wired" = runs in npm run ci / CI.
GateWhat it guarantees (plain)How (technical)Status
Asset gateEvery sprite is a real, transparent, square image small enough to shippng.mjs / validate.mjs - PNG signature, alpha channel, width=height, ≤150KBwired
Manifest schemaThe asset list is well-formed before the game reads itassets.schema.json + validateManifest()wired
Build gateThe finished playable is one self-contained file under the ad size limitvalidate-build.mjs - single HTML, ≤5MB, no external URLs, MRAID + CTAwired
Unit testsThe scoring and collision rules are proven correctnode --test via tsx - pure functionswired
End-to-end testThe built game loads and runs in a real browserPlaywright headless - no errors, loop advances, WebGL healthy, CTA fireswired
Judge rubricAn AI scores each sprite on 5 quality criteriaLLM vision judge - structured 1-5 per criterionlive: mock
AI provenanceProof each sprite was made by an AI generator, not hand-drawn or proceduralverify-provenance.mjs - each sprite’s SHA-256 matches its recorded generation fingerprintlive: 5 verified
Human escalationAnything the AI is unsure about goes to a personHITL - malformed / refused / rate-limited verdict flags humanReviewlive: 12 flagged
CI gateAll of the above run automatically on every code changenpm run ci on GitHub Actionswired

Engineering best practices - where each one lives

PracticeWhat it meansWhere
Deterministic vs judgment splitAI only where judgment is needed; code everywhere elsethe table above
Fail-loud validation at boundariesBad data is rejected and surfaced, never flows downstreamvalidate.mjs · validate-build.mjs · validateScene
Prompt as codeGeneration prompts versioned in git, reviewable, roll-back-ableprompts/*.md
Closed-loop evaluationJudge feedback rewrites the prompt until it passesthe agentic loop above
Human-in-the-loopLow-confidence / unverifiable output escalates to a personHITL - never a guess
ResilienceExponential backoff on 429/5xx; retries don't double-firejudge.mjs fetchWithBackoff
Swappable adaptersChange a provider/model without touching the pipelinegenerate.mjs · judge.mjs
ObservabilityEvery decision logged; this dashboard reads the traceruns/*.jsonl + this page
Provenance / auditEach sprite hashed and proven AI-originverify-provenance.mjs
Single source of truthOne rubric, one config, one decision point per rulerubric.mjs · run.mjs config

What's real here, and the one thing still synthetic

The whole loop is built and closed on this page: internal quality (the rubric), correctness (the gates), and real-play performance (the telemetry collector ranks variants by engagement and the champion/challenger selector recommends what to promote).

The one input still synthetic is who plays: engagement comes from automated Playwright runs, not real users, so it can't yet see true ad performance (click-through, install rate, playtime). The game emits window.__telemetry (variant, pickups, score, duration) and the collector already consumes exactly that shape, so going live is a one-line swap: point the collector at the ad network's performance webhook instead of local runs. Same shape, real users, same panels.
